mission: Determine whether the current IO boundary data can derive a nonzero leptogenesis CP asymmetry inside the already-closed type-I portal class.

theorem_1_current_boundary_data_are_cp_e.statement: On the current stack, the admitted boundary seed and transport data used in the fermionic sector are CP-even in the sense relevant to the portal coefficients: the Hawking/KMS quasi-free CAR seed is fixed by a real thermal covariance, radial transport is unitary geometric parallel transport, and no theorem-grade complex phase or chiral-selector datum is derived.

imports.seed_boundary: The current extension does not derive a preferred chirality. Without a parity-odd or chiral selector theorem, the two horizon chiral sectors remain symmetry-related.
imports.transport_boundary: After admitting the new fermionic/chiral bridge carrier, the radial support-branch transport is not a mystery object. It is the ordinary spin parallel transport on the support geometry, unitary and representation-preserving.

conditions: portal coefficients are derived only from the current CP-even boundary data
no additional CP-odd/chiral selector theorem is added
the portal class is the type-I singlet-fermion completion already closed

formulae.cp_asymmetry: epsilon1 = [1/(8 pi (Y^dagger Y)11)] sum_{j!=1} Im[(Y^dagger Y)1j^2] g(M_j^2/M1^2)
formulae.real_data_implication: Y, M can be taken real up to orthogonal basis changes

theorem_2_real_portal_completion_gives_z.statement: If the portal coefficients are built only from the current boundary data, then no theorem-grade CP-odd invariant exists from which a nonzero imaginary portal invariant can be formed. In that case Y^dagger Y is real-symmetric on the active slice and the leptogenesis numerator Im[(Y^dagger Y)1j^2] vanishes. Therefore epsilon1 = 0 on the current CP-even boundary stack.

theorem_3_exact_remaining_coefficient_ob.statement: The coefficient side does not remain an undifferentiated invariant triple. It splits exactly into: (i) CP-even portal magnitudes, represented minimally by (M1, m_tilde1), and (ii) a genuinely new CP-odd boundary theorem capable of generating epsilon1 != 0.
remaining_objects: a CP-even magnitude selector theorem for (M1, m_tilde1)
a genuinely CP-odd/chiral selector theorem producing epsilon1 != 0

final_boundary.status: derived / sharpened exhausted Paper 35 coefficient boundary
final_boundary.safe_statement: On the current boundary stack, nonzero epsilon1 is not derivable. Any positive Paper 35 coefficient closure now requires a new CP-odd boundary theorem in addition to any CP-even magnitude selector.
